common_invariants: all household, transition, workforce, rights, access, continuity, and appeal protections remain
administrative savings contribute zero to closure
the binding vote is Yes on one complete selected challenger or No on current law
construction-stage comparison among these options is not a binding multi-option ballot
a current joint score supersedes synthetic arithmetic before ballot qualification

options.change_from_common_package: raise the narrow VAT from 5.0 to 11.5 percent
options.credit_change: raise the protected-consumption credit proportionally so it equals 11.5 percent of the protected allowance
options.principal_exposure: consumers and businesses administering the VAT, net of the scaled household credit

options.change_from_common_package: add a 3 percentage-point AGI surtax above $100,000 single and $200,000 joint, indexed, and tax foreign income of U.S. corporations currently at the full statutory rate
options.principal_exposure: higher-income filers and owners, workers, and stakeholders of affected multinational corporations

options.change_from_common_package: no additional tax or program change; adopt the lower fiscal target
options.principal_exposure: future taxpayers and service users through higher debt and interest exposure than the primary-balance path

qualification_rule: No option qualifies for a public binding ballot until joint fiscal, distributional, service, legal, and implementation scores are published and any scored gap is closed within its voter-approved corridor or by newly ratified policy.
